risk_flags: Storage at 43.1% (19th percentile 5-yr) — 14.3 pp seasonal deficit raises H2 generation call if refill stalls.
Hormuz closure and Iran nuclear talks collapse risk elevate crude/diesel costs, pushing thermal marginal cost higher and reinforcing Clean Spark premium.
EU 21st sanctions package freezes Russian oil price cap; tightens alternative crude sourcing and may widen LNG arb into summer.
Jet fuel crisis easing via workarounds reduces near-term refinery pressure; risk to downside if operational fixes accelerate.

base.summary: Clean Spark and GB Power remain elevated; TTF stable mid-60s percentile; storage refill steady. Thermal in-the-money persists.

downside.summary: Jet fuel crisis fully resolves; refinery margins normalize; crude retreats; storage injection accelerates on mild weather and demand softening.

upside.summary: Iran nuclear collapse or Hormuz closure deepens; crude spikes; refined product scarcity tightens thermal marginal cost; storage injection slows.

themes: Thermal generation in-the-money; high Clean Spark and GB premium
EU storage deficit and refill pace under scrutiny
Crude and refined product supply risk from Hormuz closure and Iran escalation
top_drivers: Clean Spark at 91st-percentile (35.2 EUR/MWh) reflects tight thermal merit order and elevated gas relative to coal; storage deficit below seasonal boosts summer generation premium.
GB Power at 90th-percentile (125.69 EUR/MWh) signals UK import scarcity and wind/renewable weakness (41.7% share down 24.8% YoY); cross-channel arbitrage under pressure.
Hormuz closure and Iran escalation risk create structural bullish bias for crude and refined products, lifting thermal generation marginal cost and reinforcing power premium into summer.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 91st-percentile (35.2 EUR/MWh) and GB Power at 90th-percentile (125.69 EUR/MWh) signal tight thermal merit order; Storage 14.3 pp below seasonal pushes H2 refill urgency amid Hormuz geopolitical risk.

watchlist.why: Extended strait closure compounds EU crude scarcity and thermal generation marginal cost; directly bullish for power and Clean Spark.
At 14.3 pp seasonal deficit, injection speed will set power baseload availability and DA volatility; storage < 40% into July signals tight winter build assumption.
Collapse or escalation triggers further crude supply disruption and refined product scarcity; tail-risk to power baseload via diesel cost.
Russian oil price cap freeze reshapes crude flows and LNG arbitrage; may widen Brent-TTF or tighten EU import costs into summer.
